title: Palhinhaea maniculata
taxonomy: Palhinhaea maniculata is a species of plant in the family Hypnaceae. It belongs to the class Bryopsida. Within the class, it is further classified into the subclass Bryidae. The order is Hypnales, and the family is Hypnaceae. This classification places Palhinhaea maniculata within the division Bryophyta, also known as the mosses.
uses: Palhinhaea maniculata has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
